REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF MAGDY TAWADROUS, HANY TAWADROUS, CHRISTINE TAWADROUS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE, AS TO DEFENDANTS JENI GAMBLE AND MURRAY AGNEW IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SEEK A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO LIMIT THE NUMBER OF REQUESTS. THE OBJECTIONS BASED ON ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE, TAXPAYER PRIVILEGE AND FINANCIAL PRIVACY ARE OVERRULED. THE MOTION GRANTED AND DEFENDANTS SHALL SERVE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS WITHIN 10 DAYS. SANCTIONS AGAINST THE DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,845 ARE ALSO GRANTED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew
Off Calendar Mar-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Greg Gibbs, 415.823.4566 of T M Law Associates appearing via Zoom for Plaintiffs Magdy Tawadrous et al.
B. Douglas Robbins, 650.240.2404 of Tong Robbins LLP appear via Zoom for Defendant Jeni Gamble et al.

Hearing held. Mr. Gibbs states that the Defendant's gave generic responses and Plaintiff did not know where to within the 500 pages of documents and that the responses were not code compliant. Mr. Robbins argues that they were code compliant. They did produce the documents and told them were to look.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MAGDY TAWADROUS, HANY TAWADROUS, CHRISTINE TAWADROUS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART as follows: Defendants' responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to RFAs 1-35 are adequate. Defendants' failure to respond as to RFAs 36-50 is not justified. Defendants' are to provide responses without objections within 10 days. No sanctions are granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew
Off Calendar Mar-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Greg Gibbs, 415.823.4566 of T M Law Associates appearing via Zoom for Plaintiffs Magdy Tawadrous et al.
B. Douglas Robbins, 650.240.2404 of Tong Robbins LLP appear via Zoom for Defendant Jeni Gamble et al.

Hearing held. Mr. Robbins counsel for the Defendant contests the portion of the tentative ruling granting sanctions. The Court states that the sanction amount is reduced. Plaintiff states that sanctions are appropriate and argues that it should be more. Mr. Gibbs argues that it took time and effort prepare the motion therefore sanctions are due.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MAGDY TAWADROUS, HANY TAWADROUS, CHRISTINE TAWADROUS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew is GRANTED. Defendants failed to seek a protective order to limit the number of requests. The objections based on attorney client privilege, taxpayer privilege and financial privacy are overruled. The Motion granted and Defendants shall serve responses without objections within 10 days. Sanctions against the Defendant and Defendants' counsel in the amount of $1,845 are also granted.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew
Off Calendar Mar-07-2023 Continued To Mar-23-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRETT L. GIBBS, Esq. of TRN Law Associates (Tel: 415-823-4566) for plaintiffs;
Atty. B. DOUGLAS ROBBINS, Esq. of Tong Robbins LLP (Tel: 650-240-2404) for defendants Jennifer C. Gamble and Murray E. Agnew.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MAGDY TAWADROUS, HANY TAWADROUS, and CHRISTINE TAWADROUS' Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One, As To Defendants Jeni Gamble And Murray Agnew.

The parties do not stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge.

Matter discussed.
